name: CUSHING SYNDROME
definition: HYPERCORTISOLISM excessive glucocorticoid from either exogenous / endogenous sources autonomous cortisol production by adrenal gland

demographics.Age: 30-40 years (highest incidence); more often following pregnancy

discriminating_features: atrophy of remaining adrenal tissue ← adenoma suppresses ACTH levels

general: atrophy of remaining adrenal tissue ← adenoma suppresses ACTH levels
multiple small bilateral nodules / normal adrenal
2-4-cm focal mass in one adrenal gland + atrophy of contralateral gland = adrenal
> 4-cm large focal mass with central necrosis in one adrenal gland + atrophy of
macronodules = multinodular hyperplasia of long-standing Cushing disease
large nodules (autonomous ACTH-independent) = massive macronodular hyperplasia
small nodules = primary pigmented nodular adrenocortical disease
